title: trifolium spadiceum var. badium
intro: Trifolium spadiceum var. badium, also known as the spotted trefoil, is a variety of the Trifolium spadiceum plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the pea family. Traditionally, Trifolium spadiceum var. badium has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant are said to include the treatment of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Trifolium spadiceum var. badium is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the dried leaves and stems being used in herbal remedies.
